setting.note: An ageing German writer travels south for rest and is caught by the beauty of a boy he never speaks to, staying on as a cholera the city hushes up spreads through the summer. The map crosses the lagoon from the San Marco waterfront to the Lido, the barrier island of grand hotels and sea baths, and back into the sultry, sirocco-laden alleys of the old city.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Thomas Mann, first published 1912. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Death in Venice, Thomas Mann (1912). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or estate.

palette.note: The grey-green of the lagoon under a sirocco sky, the water Aschenbach crosses and never leaves. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
